documents.title: Press briefing, Foreign Ministry of State A
documents.text: The Spokesperson reiterated that State A will not accept any unilateral demarcation of the maritime boundary. A question on whether consultations with State B had resumed was declined. The Spokesperson added that cooperation with State C on fisheries remains "unaffected by recent tensions".

documents.title: Cable, Mission of State B
documents.text: State B's Deputy Permanent Representative stated in the corridor that "technical contacts have continued throughout" and characterized State A's posture as "rhetorical". The DPR declined to confirm a date for the next political-level meeting.

inputs.question: Extract every attributable claim in these two documents and bind it to its exact source span.

primitives.label: State A will not accept unilateral demarcation of the maritime boundary
cooperation with State C on fisheries remains unaffected by recent tensions
State A's posture characterized as rhetorical
